title: Ensure that the --audit-log-maxsize argument is set to 100 or as appropriate

input_row.title: Ensure that the --audit-log-maxsize argument is set to 100 or as appropriate
input_row.description: Rotate log files on reaching 100 MB or as appropriate.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes automatically rotates the log files. Retaining old log files ensures that you would have sufficient log data available for carrying out any investigation or correlation. If you have set file size of 100 MB and the number of old log files to keep as 10, you would approximate have 1 GB of log data that you could potentially use for your analysis.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-apiserver Verify that the --audit-log-maxsize argument is set to 100 or as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Edit the deployment specs and set --audit-log-maxsize=100 to 100 or as appropriate. kubectl edit deployments federation-apiserver-deployment -- namespace=federation-system Impact: None Default Value: By default, auditing is not enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/federation-apiserver/ 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/federation/manifests/fe deration-apiserver-deployment.yaml 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/  209 | P a g e
input_row.impact: None Default Value: By default, auditing is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_audit_log_maxsize_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the Kubernetes API to identify the deployment or static pod manifest for the federation-apiserver (typically named `federation-apiserver-deployment` in the `federation-system` namespace).
- Use the following kubectl command to retrieve the deployment specification:
  ```
  kubectl -n federation-system get deployment federation-apiserver-deployment -o json
  ```
- Parse the `spec.template.spec.containers[].command` or `spec.template.spec.containers[].args` fields to locate the `--audit-log-maxsize` argument.
- Validate that the `--audit-log-maxsize` flag is present and set to `100` (or another organization-approved value).
- **Pass Criteria:** The federation-apiserver container(s) have the `--audit-log-maxsize` argument set to `100` or a documented, appropriate value.
- **Fail Criteria:** The `--audit-log-maxsize` argument is missing or set to a value not aligned with policy (e.g., greater than 100 MB without justification).
- If the federation-apiserver is deployed as a static pod, retrieve and inspect the manifest file (e.g., `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/federation-apiserver.yaml`) via the Kubernetes API or node file system if accessible.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES 1.6 BENCHMARK V1.0.0 ARCHIVE
